segments.text: Bless the Lord, O my soul.
O Lord my God, you are very great!
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: You are clothed with splendor and majesty,
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Covering yourself with light as a garment,
stretching out the heavens like a tent.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He lays the beams of his chambers on the waters;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: he makes the clouds his chariot;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: he rides on the wings of the wind;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: he makes his messengers winds, his ministers a flaming fire.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He set the earth on its foundations, so that it should never be moved.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: Who…: Heb. He hath founded the earth upon her bases

segments.text: You covered it with the deep as with a garment; the waters stood above the mountains.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: At your rebuke they fled; at the sound of your thunder they took to flight.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The mountains rose, the valleys sank down to the place that you appointed for them.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: They go up…: or, The mountains ascend, the valleys descend

segments.text: You set a boundary that they may not pass, so that they might not again cover the earth.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You make springs gush forth in the valleys; they flow between the hills,
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: He: Heb. Who; run: Heb. walk

segments.text: giving drink to every wild animal; the wild donkeys quench their thirst.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Beside them the birds of the heavens dwell; they sing among the branches.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: From your lofty abode you water the mountains; the earth is satisfied with the fruit of your work.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You cause the grass to grow for the livestock and plants for man's cultivation, that he may bring forth food from the earth
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: and wine to gladden the heart of man, oil to make his face shine and bread to strengthen man's heart.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: oil…: Heb. to make his face shine with oil, or, more than oil

segments.text: The trees of the Lord are watered abundantly, the cedars of Lebanon that he planted,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: in which the birds build their nests, the stork has her home in the fir trees.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The high mountains are for the wild goats; the rocks are a refuge for the rock badgers.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He made the moon to mark the seasons, and the sun knows its time for setting.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You make darkness, and it is night, when all the beasts of the forest creep about.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: beasts…: Heb. beasts thereof do trample on the forest

segments.text: The young lions roar for their prey, seeking their food from God.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: When the sun rises, they steal away and lie down in their dens.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Man goes out to his work and to his labor until the evening.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: O Lord, how manifold are your works! In wisdom have you made them all; the earth is full of your creatures.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Here is the sea, great and wide, which teems with creatures innumerable, living things both small and great.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: There go the ships, and Leviathan, which you formed to play in it.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: These all look to you, to give them their food in due season.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: When you give it to them, they gather it up; when you open your hand, they are filled with good things.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: When you hide your face, they are dismayed; when you take away their breath, they die and return to their dust.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: When you send forth your Spirit, they are created, and you renew the face of the ground.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: May the glory of the Lord endure forever; may the Lord rejoice in his works,
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: who looks on the earth and it trembles, who touches the mountains and they smoke!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will sing to the Lord as long as I live; I will sing praise to my God while I have being.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: May my meditation be pleasing to him, for I rejoice in the Lord.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Let sinners be consumed from the earth, and let the wicked be no more! Bless the Lord, O my soul! Praise the Lord!
segments.speaker: Psalmist
